name: IMRC
mission: IMRC has been working for over four decades to support Muslims and minorities in India. We fund education, healthcare, water access, economic programs, and emergency relief.
theoryOfChange: IMRC states it works year round to assess needs of Muslims and minorities in poverty-struck areas of India and develop projects based on their needs, funding education, healthcare, water access, economic programs, and emergency relief through 100+ partners on the ground to create real, measurable change.
scoreSummary: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.imrcusa.org/zakat (Source: https://www.imrcusa.org/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 80 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 34/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions exceptional financial efficiency but doesn't explicitly connect it to the high program expense ratio and fundraising efficiency, which are key strengths. It also mentions medium data confidence levels but doesn't specify what contributes to this medium confidence.

The impact rationale mentions the cost per beneficiary and direct service delivery, but it doesn't mention the negative impact of the excessive working capital, which significantly reduces the impact score.
Website shows related EIN (94-2784165), likely parent/subsidiary of 27-0058132

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Zakat-collecting organization holds 80 months of reserves — zakat must be distributed promptly

risks.description: Zakat-collecting organization holds 80 months of reserves — zakat must be distributed promptly

score_details.score_summary: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.imrcusa.org/zakat (Source: https://www.imrcusa.org/zakat)

all_citations.claim: IMRC reported total revenue of $10,004,213, a program expense ratio of 89.8%, and working capital of 55.7 months.
The organization is zakat-eligible and maintains a specific zakat policy for its programs.
IMRC is profiled as a non-profit serving the needs of Indian Muslims and minorities.
The charity provides direct services including meals, water wells, and healthcare treatment.
IMRC tracks annual outcomes for grain distribution and student benefits.
The organization supported students and treated patients as part of its core programming.
IMRC has a multi-year track record of distributing meals and supporting healthcare in India.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: IMRC earns a solid 64/100 due to its exceptional financial efficiency and long-standing track record of direct service delivery in India. While its alignment with donor values is high, the overall score is moderated by medium data confidence levels.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing data confidence through more recent third-party impact evaluations
Increasing transparency regarding specific cost-per-beneficiary metrics across all program types

dimension_explanations.alignment: IMRC shows high alignment for Muslim donors by maintaining a clear zakat policy that adheres to traditional asnaf categories [2]. The organization addresses urgent advocacy and relief needs for minorities in India, supported by a long history of annual reporting on outcomes such as meals provided and water wells built [4][5][7].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates strong financial health with a total revenue of $10,004,213 and a program expense ratio of 89.8% [1]. IMRC provides direct services including healthcare treatment for thousands of patients and educational support for students, maintaining a significant reserve of 55.7 months of working capital to ensure program continuity [1][4][6].

baseline_narrative.headline: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities empowers India’s marginalized minorities through high-efficiency healthcare, education, and social security programs.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 89.8%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach beneficiaries
Exceptional fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised
Robust financial stability with 55.7 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities (IMRC) is a non-profit organization dedicated to helping India's Muslims and minorities achieve equality, freedom, and their rights as citizens [1][3]. The organization operates as a zakat-eligible entity, providing essential services including food distribution, water projects, and emergency relief [2][4].

all_citations.quote: Zakat Categories: fuqara, masakin, amil, muallaf, riqab, gharimin, fisabilillah, ibn_sabil

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: IMRC earns a solid 64/100 due to its exceptional financial efficiency and long-standing track record of direct service delivery in India. While its alignment with donor values is high, the overall score is moderated by medium data confidence levels.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization holds <cite id="13">55.7 months of working capital</cite>, which may delay the deployment of zakat funds to those in immediate need.
Revenue dropped from <cite id="4">$13.8 million</cite> to <cite id="5">$10.0 million</cite> in the most recent fiscal year, a 28% decline.

bbb_assessment.summary: BBB assessment data is not currently available for this organization.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization maintains high liquidity, which provides a buffer against economic instability in the regions served.
risk_factors: <cite id="13">68 months of operating reserves</cite> is unusually high for a humanitarian charity
<cite id="5">28% revenue decline</cite> in the most recent fiscal year
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="13">excessive accumulation of reserves</cite> and a <cite id="5">significant revenue drop</cite> in 2024.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023/2024; Impact data from 2021
confidence.notes: Strong financial transparency but impact data is slightly dated.

alignment.explanation: The charity is a strong fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="11">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and serving <cite id="12">eight distinct asnaf categories</cite>.
alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment by expanding services to <cite id="18">underserved geographies</cite> within India that have limited nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can rely on IMRC's <cite id="1">45-year track record</cite> and its <cite id="15">100% independent board of 14 members</cite>. The organization maintains <cite id="16">detailed financial records</cite> with negligible liabilities.
credibility.improvement: To improve credibility, IMRC should publish more recent third-party impact evaluations to verify long-term outcomes.

impact.explanation: IMRC delivers <cite id="6">89.8% of its budget</cite> to programs, focusing on <cite id="2">direct services</cite> like healthcare and education. Their <cite id="17">cost per beneficiary is approximately $789.88</cite> for humanitarian interventions.
impact.improvement: IMRC should <cite id="13">deploy its excess unrestricted reserves</cite> into mission delivery to increase immediate impact.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Internal outcome tracking and annual reporting

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Direct implementation through local Indian partners

rich_narrative.headline: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities empowers India’s marginalized minorities through high-efficiency healthcare, education, and social security programs.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support the Muslim minority in India through an established institution with extremely low fundraising costs.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat obligations across all eight asnaf categories
Value long-term stability and deep roots in the Indian subcontinent
Prefer organizations that spend very little on marketing and overhead
giving_considerations: Large reserves mean your donation adds to a very healthy endowment
Recent revenue declines may impact the scale of future projects
Focus is exclusively on India and its specific minority challenges
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fund rapid-growth startups or those who prefer charities with low cash reserves to ensure immediate spending.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects consistent internal reporting of outputs (meals, patients) without independent longitudinal impact studies.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: IMRC uses direct service delivery in healthcare, education, and emergency relief to alleviate poverty and promote equality for Indian minorities.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking metrics like patients treated ensures that resources are reaching the intended beneficiaries effectively.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While IMRC has high reserves, the scale of poverty in India provides significant room for expanded program delivery.

peer_comparison.differentiator: IMRC stands out for its <cite id="7">$0.01 fundraising cost</cite>, which is far below the <cite id="19">peer median efficiency</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on US-based advocacy rather than international aid

similar_organizations.differentiator: Domestic US focus with much larger scale

similar_organizations.differentiator: Smaller scale with a focus on Palestinian relief

strengths.detail: The organization spends just <cite id="7">$0.01 for every $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than industry averages.
IMRC provided medical care to <cite id="8">183,100 patients</cite> in 2021, demonstrating high-volume service delivery.
The charity maintains <cite id="13">55.7 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, ensuring long-term stability.

strengths_deep_dive: IMRC’s operational strength lies in its ability to scale basic necessity programs across India. Their food security initiatives <cite id="9">distributed 7.2 million meals</cite> in 2021, while their educational programs <cite id="14">supported 16,493 students</cite>. This breadth of service is supported by a <cite id="15">100% independent board</cite> that oversees <cite id="16">total assets of $48,785,943</cite>, providing a robust governance framework for large-scale humanitarian logistics.
rich_narrative.summary: Indian Muslim Relief and Charities (IMRC) has served as a vital lifeline for India’s marginalized minorities since its <cite id="1">founding in 1981</cite>. The organization manages a diverse portfolio of interventions, ranging from <cite id="2">emergency feeding programs</cite> to long-term <cite id="3">educational support and healthcare services</cite>. In 2023, IMRC reported <cite id="4">total revenue of $13,833,809</cite>, though the organization experienced a <cite id="5">revenue decline to $10,004,213</cite> in the following fiscal year. Despite this fluctuation, IMRC maintains a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 89.8%</cite>, directing the majority of its resources toward direct aid. The charity is exceptionally efficient in its development efforts, spending only <cite id="7">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in donations. This lean fundraising model ensures that donor contributions are not consumed by administrative overhead. IMRC’s impact is tangible across India, where it <cite id="8">treated 183,100 patients</cite> and <cite id="9">provided 7.2 million meals</cite> in a single reporting year. The organization also addresses infrastructure needs, having <cite id="10">built 809 water wells</cite> to improve rural sanitation. As a <cite id="11">zakat-eligible entity</cite>, IMRC provides a structured way for Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations, specifically serving <cite id="12">eight categories of asnaf</cite>, including the poor and needy.
